evals.prompt: Run wrapup after adding a new feature: a 40-line function in tools/sprint that validates ticket IDs. README.md was also updated to document the new behavior.
evals.expected_output: The skill runs simplifier, reviewer, and doc-audit (since README changed). Security-review is skipped (no security-sensitive patterns). repo-check runs because tools/ changed. The pipeline produces findings for each gate that ran, followed by the commit prompt.
expectations: Runs or describes running code-simplifier on the changed function
Runs or describes running doc-audit because README.md changed
Asks for confirmation before writing to doc-findings.md
Ends with a staged-files and commit message prompt asking 'Commit and push? (y to proceed)'

evals.prompt: Run wrapup. The session only changed README.md — a single sentence was reworded. No code was modified.
evals.expected_output: The skill skips code-simplifier and code-reviewer (doc-only change), skips security-review, skips repo-check (no workflow/tools/skills changed), and runs doc-audit. It explains the skip reason for each skipped gate.
expectations: Skips code-simplifier with a stated reason
Skips code-reviewer with a stated reason
States a skip reason for each skipped gate, not just silently omits them

evals.prompt: Run wrapup. The doc-audit step finds an issue in README.md. Should wrapup write it to doc-findings.md automatically?
evals.expected_output: The skill does not write to doc-findings.md automatically. It surfaces the finding and asks for confirmation ('Append these to doc-findings.md? (y to confirm)') before writing.
expectations: Does not write to doc-findings.md without confirmation
Surfaces the doc-audit finding to the user

evals.prompt: Run wrapup after a single-line fix: a typo corrected in a variable name inside tools/sprint. No docs or skills changed.
evals.expected_output: The skill applies trivial-change skip logic: skips simplifier (single-line), skips reviewer (single-line fix, no design implications), skips doc-audit (no user-facing docs changed), skips repo-check (tools changed but only a trivial rename). Ends with commit and push prompt.
expectations: Skips code-reviewer citing single-line fix with no design implications
Ends with commit and push prompt
